summary: Backend-only testing for Sistema Integral de Control Financiero y Cartera. 31/31 pytest tests PASSED (100%). Verified auth (cookie + bearer), RBAC (admin/financiero/consulta), bancos with computed disponible, cheques with bank saldo adjustments on create/update transitions, cartera with total computation, retenciones with cliente_nombre, flujo filters, dashboard KPIs and 7-day flujo, alerts prioritization, Resend email stub mode (sent:false as expected since RESEND_API_KEY is empty), and Excel exports for all 5 modules.

minor.issue: When no alerts exist returns {sent:false, reason:'Sin alertas'} without 'count' field; minor shape inconsistency.
Deleting a banco does not cascade-delete or reassign its cheques/flujo; potential orphan records.
If banco_id changes during a cobrado->cobrado update, saldo adjustments are not rebalanced across banks. Only transitions in/out of 'cobrado' are handled.
Accepts arbitrary saldo_efectivo overwrite without audit; tests use this to restore state but in prod could mask cheque-driven changes.

action_items: Consider cascade cleanup on banco deletion (reassign or block delete when linked cheques/flujo exist).
Handle cheque update when banco_id changes while estado remains 'cobrado' (reverse from old banco, apply to new).

CORS allow_origins currently set from FRONTEND_URL + localhost which is correct; however middleware is added AFTER include_router — works in FastAPI but convention is to add before. Not a bug, code-review nit.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~670 lines — consider splitting into routers/modules (auth, bancos, cheques, facturas, flujo, dashboard, export) for maintainability.
Passwords/emails hardcoded in backend/.env as plaintext seed — acceptable for demo but document rotation.
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook mentions 5-fail lockout). Not implemented.
JWT stored in httpOnly cookie with samesite='lax' and no 'secure=True' — acceptable for preview, but should be Secure in production over HTTPS.
bcrypt hash verified ($2b$) via verify_password; seed_admin is idempotent (updates hash if password changed). Good.
Dashboard endpoint loads full collections into memory (to_list 2000) — fine for demo, consider aggregation pipelines at scale.
Export endpoint has no role guard; consulta user can export all data — may or may not be intended.
test_credentials: admin@finanzas.com/admin123, financiero@finanzas.com/financiero123, consulta@finanzas.com/consulta123 — all working, seeded idempotently on startup.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded demo data (3 bancos, 6 clientes, 10 cheques, 12 facturas, 9 flujo). Tests create TEST_-prefixed data and clean up after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py covers all 31 critical backend flows. For frontend testing, login via POST /api/auth/login sets httpOnly cookie 'access_token'. Use data-testid selectors. Dashboard at /api/dashboard returns KPIs; Alerts sorted by priority (high/warning/info). Resend stub mode returns sent:false — this is EXPECTED behavior per product spec.
rca of the issue: No critical issues found. All primary flows working.
